name: Miro MCP Server
what_it_does: Miro MCP server enabling AI agents to interact with Miro whiteboards — reading and creating boards, adding sticky notes and cards, managing frames, and integrating visual collaboration data into agent workflows.
use_cases: Creating and populating Miro boards from brainstorming agents
Reading board content for synthesis and summary agents
Adding sticky notes and cards from data ingestion agents
Organizing information into frames for workshop facilitation agents
Generating visual representations of data from analytics agents
Integrating meeting notes into Miro boards from documentation agents
not_for: Teams using Mural, FigJam, or Lucidchart for collaboration
best_when: An agent needs to create or read visual boards and sticky notes in Miro — especially for brainstorming, retrospectives, or visual data organization.
avoid_when: You're using Mural, FigJam, or another whiteboard tool.

auth.notes: Miro OAuth2 apps with board-level scopes. Personal access tokens for individual use. App access tokens for organization-level.

pricing.free_tier_limits: Free: 3 editable boards, unlimited team members
pricing.notes: Generous free tier. Per-seat pricing for teams. Miro REST API available on free and paid plans. MCP server is community open source.

known_agent_gotchas: Board ID required for all operations — extract from Miro URL
Item positioning uses absolute coordinates — agents must manage layout manually
Widget types (sticky, shape, text) have different creation payloads
Board access requires team membership or explicit sharing
Large boards with many items require pagination to retrieve all content
Miro's object model (items vs widgets) changed between API versions
agent_readiness.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with board-level scopes. SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR. Enterprise SSO available.

security_score_components.security_notes: HTTPS enforced. OAuth2 with scopes. SOC 2, ISO 27001, GDPR. Enterprise SSO.
